frameworks.name: reference price theory
frameworks.one_line: Internal price standard consumers use to evaluate observed prices

frameworks.name: behavioral price research
frameworks.one_line: Experimental tradition isolating antecedents and effects of reference price

frameworks.name: panel-data brand choice modeling
frameworks.one_line: Econometric calibration of reference price models on scanner panel data

frameworks.name: integrative framework for reference price
frameworks.one_line: Organizes formation, retrieval-and-use, and influences of reference price

core_question: What does prior research reveal about the formation, retrieval, use, and downstream effects of reference price on consumer purchase decisions?
key_assumptions: Consumers form and retrieve an internal reference price when evaluating offers
Reference price effects can be studied through both behavioral experiments and panel-data models
Prior purchase history and contextual moderators shape reference price use
manipulations: Reviewed behavioral experiments varying antecedents of reference price
Reviewed panel-data calibrations of alternative reference price models
Examined moderators: purchase occasion, store promotional environment, product category
key_findings: Two distinct research streams (behavioral and modeling) have produced substantial cumulative evidence
Antecedents of reference price have measurable effects in experimental settings
Reference price influences brand choice and other purchase decisions in panel data
Prior purchase history and contextual moderators systematically shape reference price effects
interpretations: An integrative framework spanning formation, retrieval-and-use, and influences organizes the literature coherently
Unresolved issues remain and warrant a structured agenda for further research
